danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard in intestinal passages (Villi People level); immediate threat requiring avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Pointed geometric obstacles in air passages
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazard that causes Jim's balloon head to deflate and fall (Inflated Head level)

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite projectiles during balloon floating sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Active enemy aggression while Jim is in vulnerable state

danger_signals.signal: Hazards embedded in floors and walls
danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental spikes and obstacles integrated into level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Contact damage zones in intestinal/cave environments

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character sprite with distinctive silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist; final level threat in race sequence

safety_signals.visual_form: Slimy green character sprite following Jim
safety_signals.meaning: Ally asset usable as parachute or grappling tool for safe navigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprite increasing suit power to 200%
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or damage resistance state

safety_signals.visual_form: Various gun sprites (auto-aiming, Barn Blaster types)
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive tools for eliminating threats; Barn Blaster clears visible enemies
Safe state: level cleared, no immediate threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red and yellow rocket suit costume
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in multiple forms across levels (cave salamander disguise in Villi People, balloon-headed in Inflated Head)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Revealed to be cow in disguise at game end (subversion of faction identity)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final goal location in See Jim Run level
Final race opponent; pursuit threat in closing sequence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Projectile-attacking variant in Inflated Head level
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears as threat during vulnerable (balloon) state sections

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard colorful level backgrounds with defined platforms and obstacles; sprite-based enemy placement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim uses run-and-gun mechanics with varied weapon types; standard jump and movement controls

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Organic tunnel geometry with villi distortion effects; proximity-based visual warping (EU version shows varied speeds)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim forced into cave salamander disguise; must navigate without standard combat; trivia game show section replaces typical action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jim's head inflates to balloon size; upward float mechanics override normal gravity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Passive upward drift with player controlling lateral movement only; sharp objects cause deflation and reset to start; Evil the Cat actively attacks during this state

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Isometric shooter perspective (The Flyin' King level)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camera angle shifts to isometric view; Jim shown on pocket rocket vehicle; Major Mucus as distinct boss sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defend balloon bomb entity while guiding it to goal; defensive positioning replaces typical platforming

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Race sequence (See Jim Run, Run Jim Run level)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Obstacle course environment with Psy-Crow as visible competing sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forward momentum mandatory; player races to reach Princess through procedural obstacle gauntlet

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level palette inverts: non-sprite elements become grayscale (US) or gain color saturation (EU/ISO 9000); collecting second TV or changing levels reverts colors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No mechanical change; purely visual world-state shift affecting environmental perception

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprites transform or reveal cow forms; subversion of expected character identities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Non-playable state; cutscene/ending sequence where player loses agency

environmental_storytelling.example: Villi People level uses intestinal passage aesthetics paired with cave salamander disguise and trivia game show midpoint; environmental theme drives both visual and mechanical narrative
Jim's transformation into blind cave salamander in Villi People; balloon head inflation in Inflated Head; these visual changes signal player capability shifts and story progression
Major Mucus appears in isometric perspective level (The Flyin' King), indicating shift from standard platformer world-state to vehicular combat scenario
TV item grayscale/colorization effect creates distinct visual 'rooms' within same level; collected items alter player perception of environment while keeping geometry static
Game ending reveals Princess and all characters as cows in disguise; environmental assumption (rescuing human princess) contradicted by final visual state

environmental_storytelling.technique: Distortion effects as proximity danger indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Villi People level shows villi distortion increasing with proximity (EU version more pronounced); visual warping correlates with environmental hazard intensity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Runs, jumps, uses varied weapon types (auto-aiming, Barn Blaster for screen-clear); can deploy Snott as grapple/parachute tool
Restricted to cave salamander form; cannot use standard combat; forced into trivia game show participation
Passively floats upward; player controls lateral drift only; contact with sharp objects causes immediate deflation and level restart
Operates pocket rocket vehicle; must defend balloon bomb entity; positioned for isometric perspective combat rather than platforming
Follows Jim; provides slimy surface for adhesion/swinging; functions as parachute when Jim jumps; enables faster movement when carrying objects while jumping
Actively attacks Jim during vulnerable balloon state; projectile-based assault pattern

entity_behavioral_tells.state: See Jim Run, Run Jim Run level
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engaged in race competition with Jim; serves as pursuing/competing threat rather than static boss; reaches Princess-location simultaneously with Jim
Spawned hazard that detonates on contact or proximity; area-denial mechanic forcing player navigation around detonation zones

notes: Earthworm Jim 2 extensively employs level-specific mechanical and visual differentiation to communicate world-state shifts. Rather than a persistent game world with faction politics, the game uses discrete 'world states' (one per level) where visual presentation and gameplay mechanics are tightly coupled. The game's humor derives from visual subversions: the expectation of rescuing a princess subverted by the cow-disguise ending; the normal platformer world interrupted by intestinal passages and floating balloon mechanics. Danger signals are often context-specific (sharp objects only dangerous when balloon-headed; exploding sheep only appear in intestinal levels). The game communicates 'safety' primarily through power-ups and ally presence rather than environmental design. Regional differences (EU distortion effects more pronounced, color TV item mechanically different) suggest the game's visual manifestation layer was iteratively tuned for perception clarity.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Earthworm Jim 2 SNES)
